Estimates only. Uses 1.75% upfront MIP (financed), 0.55% annual MIP, ~1% property tax and ~0.6% insurance. Actual figures vary by lender, county, and your profile. Not a loan offer.

How much do you need down for an FHA loan in Florida?

FHA loans are popular with Florida buyers because the minimum down payment is just 3.5% of the purchase price for borrowers with a credit score of 580 or higher. On a $350,000 home, that's $12,250 down — far less than the 20% many people assume they need.

If your credit score is between 500 and 579, FHA still allows a loan but requires 10% down. Below 500, FHA financing generally isn't available. Use the slider above to see your down payment at any price and percentage.

Your down payment can come from gift funds

FHA permits 100% of your down payment to come from a documented gift from family, an employer, or an approved source — so you may not need to save the full amount yourself. Florida also offers down payment assistance programs that can cover part or all of it.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum FHA down payment in Florida?+

The FHA minimum is 3.5% of the purchase price if your credit score is 580 or higher. With a score of 500–579, the minimum is 10% down.

Can my FHA down payment be a gift?+

Yes. FHA allows your entire down payment to come from gift funds from family or other approved sources, as long as it's properly documented with a gift letter and paper trail.

Does the down payment include closing costs?+

No. The down payment is separate from closing costs. Closing costs typically run about 2%–5% of the loan amount, though they can sometimes be covered by seller concessions or lender credits.
